---
title: "Bayesian Statistics (PML) — Overview"
author: "Johnny van Doorn"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
---

This page collects the lecture slides and supporting materials for the master's-level **Bayesian Statistics** course (Psychological Methods Lab / PML, University of Amsterdam). The 2026 run runs 8 – 19 June, with the exam on 19 June and a group project from 22 – 26 June. Resit on 7 July.

The lectures are delivered as `reveal.js` slides; the same HTML doubles as a continuous-scroll handout — open the slide deck and press `ctrl + p` (Windows) / `cmd + p` (macOS) if you want to print to PDF.

**Recommended textbook:** Johnson, A. A., Ott, M. Q., & Dogucu, M. (2022). *Bayes Rules! An Introduction to Applied Bayesian Modeling*. Chapman & Hall / CRC. Free online: <https://www.bayesrulesbook.com>.

## Slides — 2026

| Date | Lecture | Topics | Slides |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8 Jun  | **Lecture 4** — Conjugate Models & Choosing Your Prior        | Conjugacy; selecting plausible priors; prior predictive check (Ch. 5)                                          | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture4_Conjugacy/lecture4_conjugacy.html) |
| 10 Jun | **Lecture 5** — Posterior Inference & Hypothesis Testing      | Point estimates; equal-tailed & highest-density intervals; Bayes factors; Savage–Dickey; grid + MCMC (Ch. 6–8) | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture5_GridMCMC/lecture5_gridmcmc.html) |
| 15 Jun | **Lecture 6** — Posterior Prediction & Hypothesis Specification | Prior + posterior predictive; predictive p-values; hypothesis testing as model comparison; ESP running example  | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture6_PredictionReporting/lecture6_predictionreporting.html) |
| 17 Jun | **Lecture 7** — Reporting Guidelines & Exam Preparation       | van Doorn et al. (2021) reporting checklist; five common exam mistakes; error-spotting exercises; mock exam     | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture7_ExamPrep/lecture7_examprep.html) |

## Course schedule

| Date | What |
|---|---|
| 8 Jun  | Lecture 4 — Conjugacy + prior selection |
| 10 Jun | Lecture 5 — Posterior inference + hypothesis testing |
| 15 Jun | Lecture 6 — Posterior prediction + hypothesis specification |
| 17 Jun | Lecture 7 — Reporting guidelines + exam prep |
| 19 Jun | **Exam** |
| 22 – 26 Jun | Group project |
| 7 Jul | Resit |

## Key references

- **Bayes Rules!** (Johnson, Ott & Dogucu, 2022) — main textbook
- **van Doorn, J. B. et al. (2021).** *The JASP guidelines for conducting and reporting a Bayesian analysis.* *Psychonomic Bulletin & Review*, 28, 813 – 826. <https://doi.org/10.3758/s13423-020-01798-5>
- **Etz, A., Haaf, J. M., Rouder, J. N., & Vandekerckhove, J. (2018).** *Bayesian inference and testing any hypothesis you can specify.* *Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science*, 1(2), 281 – 295. <https://doi.org/10.1177/2515245918773087>

## Related courses

- [SSR Overview](../SSR/OverviewSSR.qmd) — Scientific & Statistical Reasoning (bachelor)
- [RMS Overview](../RMS/OverviewRMS.qmd) — Research Methods & Statistics
- [Paddling Pool](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/paddling-pool/) — modular JASP refresher for master's students

## Contact

For questions, errata or suggestions, email Johnny van Doorn at [j.b.vandoorn@uva.nl](mailto:j.b.vandoorn@uva.nl).
